licenses.name: Business Source License 1.1
licenses.description: Source-available but not open source: restricts production use until a specified Change Date, after which a designated open-source license applies.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by MariaDB, HashiCorp (Terraform), CockroachDB. NOT open source — it is 'source available'. Converts to an open-source license (e.g., Apache-2.0) after the Change Date (usually 4 years). Commercial production use requires a commercial license before that date.

licenses.name: Elastic License 2.0
licenses.description: Source-available license that prohibits providing the software as a managed service and circumventing license key restrictions.

metadata.explanation_hint: Used by Elasticsearch 7.11+ and Kibana. Allows internal and commercial use, but you CANNOT offer it as a SaaS/cloud service. Created in response to AWS re-licensing concerns. NOT open source by OSI definition.

licenses.name: Proprietary / All Rights Reserved
licenses.description: No rights granted beyond what the license explicitly states. Default copyright law applies — use, distribution, and modification require explicit permission.

metadata.explanation_hint: Default when no license is specified — all rights reserved by the author. Others cannot legally use, copy, modify, or distribute. Add a license file to allow any use.
